services.name: Lambda
services.description: In a serverless context, Lambda is the compute engine that glues everything together. Combined with API Gateway, DynamoDB, and other managed services, it forms complete applications with zero server management.
keyPoints: Event sources: 200+ AWS service integrations
Destinations: route async results to SQS, SNS, Lambda, EventBridge
Powertools: structured logging, tracing, metrics for serverless
Container image support: package Lambda as Docker images (up to 10 GB)
SnapStart: pre-initialize for Java/Python cold start reduction
examTips: Async invocation: Lambda retries twice on failure, then sends to DLQ/destination
Reserved concurrency: guarantees capacity but limits max. Provisioned: eliminates cold starts
Lambda in VPC: needs NAT Gateway for internet access (adds cold start latency)
Execution context reuse: initialize SDK clients outside the handler
Lambda Layers: share libraries across functions (up to 5 layers per function)

quiz.q: How many times does Lambda retry an asynchronous invocation on failure?
quiz.explanation: For asynchronous invocations, Lambda automatically retries twice with delays between retries. Failed events can go to a DLQ or destination.

services.name: Step Functions
services.description: Serverless orchestration service that lets you combine Lambda functions and other AWS services into business-critical workflows. Visual editor with state machine execution.
keyPoints: State types: Task, Choice, Parallel, Wait, Map, Pass, Succeed, Fail
Standard workflows: long-running (up to 1 year), exactly-once execution
Express workflows: high-volume, short-duration (up to 5 min), at-least-once
Built-in error handling: retry policies and catch blocks
200+ AWS service integrations (direct SDK calls, no Lambda needed)
examTips: Standard: guaranteed exactly-once, $0.025 per 1,000 transitions. Express: at-least-once, $0.00001 per request
Map state: run same steps for each item in a collection (parallel iteration)
Choice state: branching logic (like if/else) based on input
Activity tasks: pause workflow and wait for external worker
Optimized integrations: call AWS services directly without Lambda glue

quiz.q: What is the maximum duration of a Standard Step Functions workflow?
quiz.explanation: Standard workflows can run for up to 1 year. Express workflows are limited to 5 minutes but are cheaper for high-volume, short-duration use cases.

services.name: EventBridge
services.tagline: Serverless event bus for decoupled architectures
services.description: Serverless event bus that makes it easy to connect applications using events. Route events from AWS services, custom apps, and SaaS partners to targets.
keyPoints: Event buses: default (AWS events), custom (your apps), partner (SaaS)
Rules: match events by pattern and route to targets
Schema registry: discover and manage event schemas
Scheduler: cron and rate-based scheduling (replaces CloudWatch Events)
Archive and replay: store events and replay them later
examTips: EventBridge is the evolution of CloudWatch Events (same underlying service)
Content-based filtering: match on any field in the event JSON
Up to 5 targets per rule (use SNS fan-out for more)
Cross-account and cross-region event delivery supported
EventBridge Pipes: point-to-point integrations with filtering and enrichment

quiz.q: What is EventBridge's relationship to CloudWatch Events?
options: EventBridge is the evolution of CloudWatch Events
quiz.explanation: EventBridge evolved from CloudWatch Events and uses the same underlying API. EventBridge adds partner integrations, schema registry, and archive/replay.

services.name: SQS
services.description: Fully managed message queuing service for decoupling and scaling microservices. Send, store, and receive messages between components at any volume.
keyPoints: Standard queue: unlimited throughput, at-least-once delivery, best-effort ordering
FIFO queue: exactly-once processing, strict ordering (300 msgs/sec, 3000 with batching)
Visibility timeout: prevent other consumers from processing a message (default 30s)
Dead-letter queue (DLQ): capture messages that fail processing after N attempts
Long polling: reduce empty responses and cost (wait up to 20 seconds)
examTips: Max message size: 256 KB. For larger, use SQS Extended Client Library (stores payload in S3)
Message retention: 1 minute to 14 days (default 4 days)
FIFO queues: message group ID for ordering, deduplication ID for exactly-once
Lambda polls SQS automatically — no need for custom polling code
Delay queue: postpone delivery of new messages (0 to 15 minutes)

quiz.q: What is the maximum message size in SQS?
quiz.explanation: SQS messages can be up to 256 KB. For larger payloads, use the SQS Extended Client Library which stores the body in S3 and sends a pointer.

quiz.q: How do you ensure exactly-once processing with SQS?
quiz.explanation: FIFO queues provide exactly-once processing using deduplication IDs. Standard queues provide at-least-once delivery (possible duplicates).

services.name: SNS
services.description: Fully managed pub/sub messaging service for application-to-application (A2A) and application-to-person (A2P) communication.
keyPoints: Topics: named channels for grouping messages
A2A subscribers: SQS, Lambda, HTTP/S, Kinesis Data Firehose
A2P subscribers: SMS, email, mobile push notifications
Message filtering: subscribers receive only matching messages
FIFO topics: strict ordering paired with SQS FIFO queues
examTips: SNS + SQS fan-out: one SNS topic → multiple SQS queues for parallel processing
Message filtering: reduces processing by routing only relevant messages to each subscriber
SNS does NOT retain messages — if subscriber is down, message is lost (use SQS for durability)
Cross-region SNS subscriptions are supported
SNS FIFO + SQS FIFO: ordered fan-out with deduplication

quiz.q: What happens if an SNS subscriber is unavailable when a message is published?
options: Message is stored until subscriber is ready
Message is lost after retry attempts
quiz.explanation: SNS retries delivery based on the delivery policy, but does not persist messages. For guaranteed delivery, pair SNS with SQS (fan-out pattern).

services.name: AppSync
services.description: Fully managed service for building GraphQL and Pub/Sub APIs. Connects to DynamoDB, Lambda, HTTP, and other data sources with real-time subscriptions.
keyPoints: GraphQL: query, mutation, and subscription operations
Data sources: DynamoDB, Lambda, HTTP, RDS, OpenSearch, EventBridge
Resolvers: map GraphQL operations to data sources (VTL or JavaScript)
Real-time subscriptions: WebSocket-based live updates
Caching: server-side response caching for performance
examTips: AppSync vs API Gateway: AppSync for GraphQL, API Gateway for REST/HTTP
Conflict resolution: for offline-first apps with data sync
Pipeline resolvers: chain multiple data sources in a single query
Merged APIs: combine multiple AppSync APIs into one endpoint
Built-in Cognito integration for authentication

quiz.q: What type of API does AWS AppSync primarily provide?
quiz.explanation: AWS AppSync is a managed GraphQL service, providing query, mutation, and real-time subscription capabilities.
